summary: Non-paper target-point feedback was removed. With the Clark paper Setting 1 dynamics, lambda=1e-6, c=1, dt=1 ms, and distances measured to the original target manifold, the current DANDI-derived target manifold is locally unstable and trajectories diverge.

diagnostic_observations.unit_selection: 31 mice and 1533 QC-passing excitatory head-direction cells, matching the paper count.
diagnostic_observations.wake_opto: NWB epochs separate wake_square and wake_opto; current extraction uses wake_square only.
diagnostic_observations.target_point_feedback: A previous diagnostic-only feedback gain produced an artificially flat trace and has been removed because it is not in the Clark paper.
diagnostic_observations.regularization_grid: Increasing lambda to about 1e-4 to 1e-3 reduces divergence and produces slow drift, but Appendix B4.4 states that larger lambda stabilizes a different, smaller-radius manifold, so this is not used as the default reproduction.

next_debug_targets: Compare preprocessing against the paper/code at the level of tuning-curve arrays, not only unit counts.
Check whether Clark used a released tuning matrix or extra smoothing/postprocessing not captured by the NWB reconstruction.
If keeping the reconstructed NWB target, report the lambda-dependence as a reproduction discrepancy rather than silently changing lambda.
